body.title: Surface low/negative confidence memories in day-close-audit
body.result: registered negative-confidence-memories SQL audit check in audit_checks.json; fires as LOW finding at day-close
body.description:
Once confidence exists on clan.memories, make it visible at day-close. Add a step (or extend an existing step) in day-close-audit that queries for: (1) PROCEDURAL memories with confidence < 0 (actively harmful habits — flag for human review), (2) SEMANTIC memories with confidence < 0 (wrong facts — flag for correction). Surface as LOW findings in the audit report. This closes the Shankar feedback loop: the system surfaces what it knows it got wrong.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/day_close_audit.py or the registered audit_checks.json (new check), psql query
**Design rules:** theigors/rules/database
**Scope boundary:** In scope: read-only query + audit report entry. Out of scope: auto-correcting or deleting negative-confidence memories.
**Test plan:** Seed one memory with confidence=-0.5; verify audit step returns it in findings. Seed only null-confidence memories; verify no findings.
